questions.prompt: 'Podes confiar ___ mim, não conto a ninguém.'
questions.explanation: 'Confiar em' = to trust (in) someone.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is correct in European Portuguese?
questions.explanation: In Portugal 'precisar' keeps its 'de' even before a noun: preciso de ajuda.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ela apaixonou-se ___ um colega de trabalho.' (preposition)
questions.explanation: 'Apaixonar-se por' = to fall in love with.

questions.prompt: Complete with preposition + article: 'O sucesso depende ___ trabalho de todos.'
questions.explanation: 'Depender de' + o trabalho contracts to 'do': depende do trabalho.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'Ainda não me habituei a acordar cedo.'
questions.explanation: 'Habituar-se a' + infinitive; 'não' pulls the pronoun forward (próclise).

activities.title: Novidades do trabalho
activities.intro: A friend grills you about your new job — and a certain someone at the office. Play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então, como vai o novo emprego? Já te habituaste ao horário?
lines.en: So, how's the new job going? Have you got used to the schedule yet?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ainda não me habituei a acordar às seis, mas gosto do trabalho.
lines.en: I still haven't got used to waking up at six, but I like the job.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E dizem que te apaixonaste por alguém do escritório...
lines.en: And they say you've fallen in love with someone from the office...

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quem te contou isso?! Bem... é verdade, penso nela todos os dias.
lines.en: Who told you that?! Well... it's true, I think about her every day.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Eu sabia! E podes confiar nos colegas para guardar o segredo?
lines.en: I knew it! And can you trust your colleagues to keep the secret?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não confio em ninguém ali! Agora tudo depende de eu ter coragem.
lines.en: I don't trust anyone there! Now it all depends on me having courage.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Não desistas! Convida-a para um café.
lines.en: Don't give up! Invite her for a coffee.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tens razão. Amanhã atrevo-me a falar com ela.
lines.en: You're right. Tomorrow I'll dare to talk to her.

days.title: Clíticos: revisão
days.objective: Master ênclise vs próclise and the contracted double pronouns mo, to, lho.
days.paretoFocus: Clitic placement is THE sound of European Portuguese — chamo-me vs não me chamo — and the contractions mo/to/lho appear constantly in real speech.
drills: Introduce yourself and your routine with five ênclise verbs: chamo-me, levanto-me, deito-me...
Negate each of them: 'Não me chamo..., nunca me deito antes das onze...'.
Practise the contractions aloud: deu-mo, dei-to, contei-lho.

items: Recall five próclise triggers — words that pull the pronoun in front of the verb.
Say the contractions: me + o, te + o, lhe + o.
Translate: 'I want you to tell me the truth.'

activities.title: Antes ou depois do verbo?
activities.intro: Ênclise (verb-pronoun) is the European default; negation, 'que', question words and adverbs like 'já' force próclise. And two pronouns can merge into one.

items.hr: Chamo-me Rita e deito-me sempre cedo.
items.en: My name is Rita and I always go to bed early.
items.note: ênclise = the default in affirmative main clauses

items.hr: Não me chamo Maria — enganaste-te no nome.
items.en: My name isn't Maria — you got the name wrong.
items.note: negation forces próclise: não me chamo

items.hr: O bebé já se deitou; fala baixo.
items.en: The baby has already gone to bed; keep your voice down.
items.note: já, ainda, também, só, sempre attract the pronoun

items.hr: Quero que me digas a verdade.
items.en: I want you to tell me the truth.
items.note: the conjunction 'que' forces próclise in the subordinate clause

items.hr: Deste-lhe o recado? — Sim, dei-lho ao almoço.
items.en: Did you give him the message? — Yes, I gave it to him at lunch.
items.note: lhe + o = lho

items.hr: Ele emprestou-me o carro — emprestou-mo por uma semana.
items.en: He lent me the car — he lent it to me for a week.
items.note: me + o = mo

items.hr: Pediste-me o dicionário e eu dei-to logo.
items.en: You asked me for the dictionary and I gave it to you straight away.
items.note: te + o = to

items.hr: Vou levar as chaves. → Vou levá-las já.
items.en: I'm going to take the keys. → I'm going to take them now.
items.note: after infinitive -r: levar + as → levá-las

activities.title: Pronomes no sítio certo
activities.intro: Default ênclise, forced próclise and the merged pronouns — place every clitic correctly.

questions.prompt: Choose the European default for introducing yourself:
questions.explanation: In an affirmative main clause the pronoun follows the verb with a hyphen: ênclise.

questions.prompt: Which is correct with 'já'?
questions.explanation: 'Já' is an attractor adverb: it forces próclise — já se deitou, never já deitou-se.

questions.prompt: 'Deste o recado ao chefe?' — 'Sim, dei-___ de manhã.' Combine lhe + o:
questions.explanation: lhe + o contracts to 'lho'; likewise me + o = mo and te + o = to.

questions.prompt: Rewrite with the clitic: 'Vou levar as chaves.' → 'Vou ___ já.' (levar + as)
questions.explanation: The infinitive drops -r and the pronoun becomes -las: levá-las.

questions.prompt: Complete with the pronoun: 'Não ___ posso dizer o segredo agora.' (to you, informal)
questions.explanation: Negation forces próclise: não te posso dizer.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'Quero que me digas a verdade.'
questions.explanation: 'Que' forces próclise (que me digas) and 'querer que' takes the conjuntivo (digas).

activities.title: O segredo
activities.intro: Your friend is dying to know a secret you promised to keep. Play 'Me' and hold the line — with perfect clitics.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então, já sabes a novidade da Marta?
lines.en: So, do you already know Marta's news?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sei, mas prometi que não a contava a ninguém.
lines.en: I do, but I promised I wouldn't tell anyone.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vá lá, conta-ma! Eu guardo segredo, juro.
lines.en: Come on, tell it to me! I'll keep the secret, I swear.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não ta posso contar, prometi mesmo.
lines.en: I can't tell it to you, I really promised.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E se eu adivinhar? Ela vai casar-se, não vai?
lines.en: And if I guess? She's getting married, isn't she?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não te digo nada! Pergunta-lhe a ela.
lines.en: I'm not telling you anything! Ask her yourself.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Está bem, pergunto-lho hoje à noite.
lines.en: Fine, I'll ask her about it tonight.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Boa sorte — mas não digas que fui eu!
lines.en: Good luck — but don't say it was me!

days.title: Conectores do discurso
days.objective: Structure a spoken opinion with porém, no entanto, além disso, por isso, portanto, ou seja and aliás.
days.paretoFocus: Seven connectors turn isolated sentences into an argument — exactly what the B1 oral exam rewards and what makes you sound organised in any discussion.
drills: Give a one-minute opinion on city life using porém, além disso and por isso.
Rephrase three ideas with 'ou seja': say it once, then say it more simply.
Contrast two restaurants using 'no entanto' and 'apesar de'.

items: Recall which connectors mark contrast, addition and consequence.
Say the difference between 'ou seja' and 'aliás'.
Translate: 'The flat is small; however, it is very bright.'

activities.title: Ligar as ideias
activities.intro: Each connector has one job: contrast, addition, consequence, rephrasing or reinforcement. Master the seven big ones.

items.hr: Estudei imenso; porém, o exame correu mal.
items.en: I studied a lot; however, the exam went badly.
items.note: porém = however (contrast)

items.hr: O restaurante é caro; no entanto, vale a pena.
items.en: The restaurant is expensive; however, it's worth it.
items.note: no entanto = nevertheless (contrast, very common in speech)

items.hr: A comida é ótima; além disso, o serviço é rápido.
items.en: The food is great; besides, the service is fast.
items.note: além disso = besides, moreover (addition)

items.hr: Estava a chover, por isso ficámos em casa.
items.en: It was raining, so we stayed at home.
items.note: por isso = so, that's why (consequence)

items.hr: Não tenho dinheiro; portanto, não vou de férias este ano.
items.en: I have no money; therefore, I'm not going on holiday this year.
items.note: portanto = therefore (conclusion, slightly more formal)

items.hr: Ele é autarca, ou seja, trabalha na câmara municipal.
items.en: He's a local official, that is, he works at the town hall.
items.note: ou seja = that is / in other words (rephrasing)

items.hr: Gosto de Lisboa; aliás, é a minha cidade preferida.
items.en: I like Lisbon; in fact, it's my favourite city.
items.note: aliás = in fact / by the way (reinforces or corrects)

items.hr: Apesar de estar cansada, foi ao ginásio.
items.en: Despite being tired, she went to the gym.
items.note: apesar de + infinitive = despite

activities.title: O conector certo
activities.intro: Contrast, consequence or rephrasing? Choose the connector that fits the logic.

questions.prompt: 'O filme era longo; ___, ninguém saiu da sala.' (contrast)
questions.explanation: The second idea contradicts the expectation, so a contrast connector fits: no entanto.

questions.prompt: 'Estava doente, ___ fiquei em casa.' (consequence)
questions.explanation: Being ill causes staying home — 'por isso' introduces the consequence.

questions.prompt: 'Ele é tradutor, ___, passa livros do inglês para português.' (rephrasing)
questions.explanation: 'Ou seja' rephrases the same idea in other words; 'isto é' works the same way.

questions.prompt: Complete with the addition connector: 'O hotel é barato; ___ disso, fica no centro.'
questions.explanation: 'Além disso' = besides / moreover, adding a second argument in the same direction.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Trabalhou a vida toda; ___, merece a reforma.' (therefore)
questions.explanation: 'Portanto' draws the logical conclusion from what came before.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'Apesar de estar cansado, fui ao ginásio.'
questions.explanation: 'Apesar de' + infinitive opens the concession; the main clause follows after the comma.

activities.title: Campo ou cidade?
activities.intro: A friend asks where life is better. Play 'Me' and build a structured opinion, connector by connector.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Achas que se vive melhor no campo ou na cidade?
lines.en: Do you think life is better in the countryside or in the city?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Na cidade há mais emprego; além disso, os transportes são melhores.
lines.en: In the city there are more jobs; besides, public transport is better.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: É verdade. No entanto, as rendas estão caríssimas.
lines.en: That's true. However, rents are extremely expensive.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pois estão, por isso muita gente se muda para os arredores.
lines.en: They really are, which is why many people move to the outskirts.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ou seja, ficas com casa mais barata e apanhas o comboio para o centro.
lines.en: In other words, you get a cheaper house and take the train into the centre.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Exatamente. Aliás, estou a pensar fazer isso no próximo ano.
lines.en: Exactly. In fact, I'm thinking of doing that next year.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A sério? Portanto, vais trocar Lisboa por Sintra!
lines.en: Seriously? So you're going to swap Lisbon for Sintra!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Talvez! Apesar de adorar a cidade, preciso de mais sossego.
lines.en: Maybe! Despite loving the city, I need more peace and quiet.

days.title: Revisão B1 I
days.objective: Consolidate the B1 verb system: presente do conjuntivo, futuro and condicional, and the compound tenses tenho feito / tinha feito.
days.paretoFocus: These four structures are the engine of B1 — reactivating them together in mixed exercises cements them far better than reviewing each in isolation.
drills: Recite the seven irregular conjuntivos: seja, esteja, vá, dê, saiba, queira, haja.
Make three wishes for next year with 'Espero que...' + conjuntivo.
Tell a short story that uses 'já tinha + particípio' twice.

items: Recall the conjuntivo triggers: querer que, é preciso que, talvez, embora.
Say the difference between 'tenho estudado' and 'estudei'.
Translate: 'When I arrived, the train had already left.'

activities.title: O sistema verbal do B1
activities.intro: One key sentence per structure — read each one, cover it, and rebuild it from memory.

items.hr: Quero que venhas jantar cá a casa no sábado.
items.en: I want you to come to dinner at my place on Saturday.
items.note: querer que + presente do conjuntivo (vir → venhas)

items.hr: Talvez chova amanhã; leva o guarda-chuva.
items.en: Maybe it will rain tomorrow; take the umbrella.
items.note: talvez + conjuntivo (chover → chova)

items.hr: Embora seja caro, o restaurante vale a pena.
items.en: Although it is expensive, the restaurant is worth it.
items.note: embora + conjuntivo; seja is one of the seven irregulars

items.hr: Um dia falarei português na perfeição.
items.en: One day I will speak Portuguese perfectly.
items.note: futuro simples: infinitive + -ei/-ás/-á/-emos/-ão

items.hr: Gostaria de marcar uma consulta para amanhã de manhã.
items.en: I would like to book an appointment for tomorrow morning.
items.note: condicional for polite requests: gostaria, poderia

items.hr: Ultimamente tenho estudado todos os dias.
items.en: Lately I have been studying every day.
items.note: pretérito perfeito composto = repetition up to now, with 'ultimamente'

items.hr: Quando cheguei à estação, o comboio já tinha partido.
items.en: When I arrived at the station, the train had already left.
items.note: mais-que-perfeito composto = past before another past

activities.title: Tudo misturado
activities.intro: Conjuntivo, condicional and the compound tenses side by side — choose the form the sentence demands.

questions.prompt: 'É preciso que tu ___ na estação às nove em ponto.'
questions.explanation: 'É preciso que' triggers the presente do conjuntivo; estar is irregular: esteja, estejas.

questions.prompt: 'No teu lugar, eu ___ com o senhorio antes de assinar.' (conditional)
questions.explanation: Hypothetical advice ('in your place') takes the condicional: falaria.

questions.prompt: 'Duvido que ele ___ mudar de emprego.'
questions.explanation: 'Duvidar que' expresses doubt, so it takes the conjuntivo; querer → queira is irregular.

questions.prompt: Complete with the pretérito perfeito composto: 'Este mês ___ trabalhado demasiado.' (ter, eu)
questions.explanation: Repeated action up to the present: presente de ter + particípio — tenho trabalhado.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Quando cheguei ao cinema, o filme já ___ começado.' (ter, mais-que-perfeito)
questions.explanation: A past before another past: imperfeito de ter + particípio — tinha começado.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'Espero que tenhas um bom fim de semana.'
questions.explanation: 'Esperar que' triggers the conjuntivo: ter → tenhas.

activities.title: Férias nos Açores
activities.intro: A friend is planning a group trip to the Azores. Play 'Me' — wishes, doubts and plans all mix the B1 tenses.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Espero que possas vir connosco aos Açores em setembro.
lines.en: I hope you can come with us to the Azores in September.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Talvez vá, mas é preciso que o chefe me dê os dias de férias.
lines.en: Maybe I'll go, but my boss needs to give me the days off.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Fala com ele já. Quando pedi as minhas, os voos baratos já tinham desaparecido.
lines.en: Talk to him now. When I asked for mine, the cheap flights had already disappeared.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tens razão. Ultimamente tenho trabalhado imenso; mereço um descanso.
lines.en: You're right. Lately I've been working loads; I deserve a rest.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nesse caso, gostaria de reservar a casa ainda esta semana.
lines.en: In that case, I'd like to book the house this very week.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Embora setembro esteja longe, é melhor que trates disso agora.
lines.en: Although September is far off, it's better that you deal with it now.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vou reservar hoje à noite. Depois digo-te o preço.
lines.en: I'll book tonight. Then I'll tell you the price.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perfeito. Duvido que encontres coisa melhor do que aquela casa!
lines.en: Perfect. I doubt you'll find anything better than that house!

days.title: Revisão B1 II
days.objective: Consolidate B1 themes and tools — bureaucracy, media and environment vocabulary woven with relatives, reported speech and connectors — and self-assess with a can-do checklist.
days.paretoFocus: Mixing themes with structures is exactly what real conversation demands; the can-do check tells you honestly whether the B1 block has landed.
drills: Simulate a Finanças visit: greet formally, explain your problem, ask the clerk to repeat.
Summarise a telejornal news story, then report it: 'O jornalista disse que...'.
Argue for recycling in four sentences, each starting with a different connector.

items: Can-do check: report what a friend said yesterday using 'disse que' with the tense shifted back — without hesitating.
Can-do check: give a one-minute opinion on recycling using porém, além disso and ou seja.
Can-do check: describe your neighbourhood with que, onde and cujo — if any of the three fails, revisit the relative pronouns day before moving on.

activities.title: O B1 em sete frases
activities.intro: Seven sentences, each combining a B1 theme with a B1 structure. Read, understand, then rebuild each from its English.

items.hr: Marquei atendimento nas Finanças para tratar dos impostos.
items.en: I booked an appointment at the tax office to deal with my taxes.
items.note: bureaucracy: marcar atendimento, tratar de

items.hr: O jornalista disse que a notícia tinha sido confirmada por duas fontes.
items.en: The journalist said the news had been confirmed by two sources.
items.note: media + reported speech + passive, all in one

items.hr: O documentário cujo tema são as alterações climáticas ganhou um prémio.
items.en: The documentary whose subject is climate change won a prize.
items.note: cujo + media + environment

items.hr: O ecoponto onde deixamos o vidro fica ao fundo da rua.
items.en: The recycling point where we leave the glass is at the end of the street.
items.note: onde + environment vocabulary

items.hr: A reciclagem dá trabalho; no entanto, é essencial para o ambiente.
items.en: Recycling takes effort; however, it is essential for the environment.
items.note: connector 'no entanto' structuring an opinion

items.hr: A funcionária pediu-me para preencher o formulário e assinar no fim.
items.en: The clerk asked me to fill in the form and sign at the end.
items.note: reported request: pedir para + infinitivo

items.hr: Depende de ti: podes desistir ou continuar a esforçar-te.
items.en: It depends on you: you can give up or keep making an effort.
items.note: regência recap: depender de, desistir, esforçar-se

activities.title: Prova geral do bloco
activities.intro: Everything at once: relatives, reported speech, connectors and the passive across the B1 themes.

questions.prompt: 'O documentário ___ tema é o ambiente ganhou um prémio.'
questions.explanation: 'Cujo' expresses possession and agrees with 'o tema': the documentary whose subject.

questions.prompt: How do you report the clerk's request «Preencha o formulário.»?
options: A funcionária pediu-me para preencher o formulário.
A funcionária disse que preencho o formulário.
A funcionária perguntou se preenche o formulário.
A funcionária pediu que preencho o formulário.
questions.answer: A funcionária pediu-me para preencher o formulário.
questions.explanation: Requests and commands are reported with 'pedir para + infinitivo' in spoken pt-PT.

questions.prompt: 'Separo sempre o vidro; ___, levo o papel ao ecoponto.' (addition)
questions.explanation: A second argument in the same direction needs the addition connector 'além disso'.

questions.prompt: Complete the reported speech: 'A jornalista disse que a manchete ___ sido escrita à pressa.' (ter)
questions.explanation: Reported past-before-past: mais-que-perfeito composto — tinha sido escrita (also passive!).

questions.prompt: Complete with the place relative: 'A junta de freguesia ___ tratei dos documentos fica perto do mercado.'
questions.explanation: 'Onde' = the place relative: the parish office where I sorted out my documents.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'A proposta foi aceite por todos os vizinhos.'
questions.explanation: Passive with the pt-PT participle 'aceite' and the agent introduced by 'por'.

activities.title: Balanço de um ano
activities.intro: A Portuguese friend asks how far your Portuguese has come. Play 'Me' — this is your informal B1 self-assessment.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Já estás em Portugal há um ano. Sentes que o teu português melhorou?
lines.en: You've been in Portugal for a year now. Do you feel your Portuguese has improved?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sem dúvida. Ontem percebi o telejornal todo, o que era impossível há meses.
lines.en: Without a doubt. Yesterday I understood the whole TV news, which was impossible months ago.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E as coisas práticas? Consegues tratar de tudo nas Finanças?
lines.en: And the practical stuff? Can you handle everything at the tax office?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Consigo. A funcionária pediu-me para preencher um formulário e percebi tudo.
lines.en: I can. The clerk asked me to fill in a form and I understood everything.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E quando alguém fala depressa? O que é que fazes?
lines.en: And when someone speaks fast? What do you do?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Peço à pessoa para repetir; ou seja, já não finjo que percebo tudo.
lines.en: I ask the person to repeat; in other words, I no longer pretend I understand everything.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Disseste-me que querias ler um romance português. Já começaste?
lines.en: You told me you wanted to read a Portuguese novel. Have you started?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Comecei um livro cujo autor é do Porto. Custa; no entanto, não desisto.
lines.en: I started a book whose author is from Porto. It's hard; however, I'm not giving up.

days.title: DEPLE: Compreensão da Leitura
days.objective: Tackle the DEPLE reading paper with a strategy: skim announcements, letters and short articles, hunt for the deciding detail, and spot distractor traps.
days.paretoFocus: Reading is 25% of the DEPLE and the most trainable component: reading the questions first and scanning for names, dates and numbers wins most of the marks before you even read the full text.
drills: Skim any Portuguese announcement online and answer in one sentence: quem, quando, onde, quanto?
Read a short pt-PT news item and give it a one-line title in Portuguese.
Invent two plausible wrong options ('distratores') for the question 'Quando reabre a biblioteca?' and explain each trap.

items: Recall: in the exam, what should you read first — the text or the questions?
Say three text types that appear in the DEPLE reading paper: anúncios, cartas, artigos curtos.
Translate: 'The library reopens on the 21st of September.'

activities.title: Estratégias de leitura para o exame
activities.intro: The DEPLE reading paper uses short authentic texts: classified ads, formal letters, notices and brief articles. Learn the strategy lines and the text-type formulas you will meet.

items.hr: Leia primeiro as perguntas: assim já sabe o que procurar no texto.
items.en: Read the questions first: that way you already know what to look for in the text.
items.note: core exam strategy — the questions tell you which details matter

items.hr: Vende-se T2 remodelado perto do metro. Contactar depois das 18h.
items.en: For sale: renovated two-bedroom flat near the metro. Contact after 6 pm.
items.note: classified ads are telegraphic — scan for numbers and times

items.hr: Exmo. Senhor Diretor, venho por este meio apresentar uma reclamação.
items.en: Dear Director, I hereby wish to make a complaint.
items.note: formal letter opening — it signals register and purpose in one line

items.hr: Procure nomes, datas, números e locais: as respostas escondem-se nos detalhes.
items.en: Look for names, dates, numbers and places: the answers hide in the details.
items.note: most reading questions are decided by a single small detail

items.hr: Cuidado com os distratores: usam palavras do texto, mas mudam um detalhe importante.
items.en: Beware of distractors: they use words from the text but change one important detail.
items.note: if an option repeats the text almost word for word, check it twice

items.hr: A partir de segunda-feira, o autocarro 23 passa a ter novo percurso.
items.en: From Monday, bus 23 will have a new route.
items.note: public notices often hinge on 'a partir de' — the date is the answer

activities.title: Leitura tipo DEPLE
activities.intro: Exam-style reading. First read the text, then answer as in the exam — one detail decides each option. TEXT: 'A Biblioteca Municipal de Coimbra vai estar encerrada entre 2 e 20 de setembro para obras de modernização. Durante este período, os leitores podem devolver os livros na carrinha da biblioteca itinerante, estacionada ao lado do mercado municipal, de segunda a sexta, das 10h às 17h. Os empréstimos são automaticamente prolongados até ao dia da reabertura, pelo que não há multas para quem entregar mais tarde. A sala de estudo do primeiro andar continuará aberta aos estudantes, mas apenas com marcação prévia através do sítio da biblioteca. Pedimos desculpa pelo incómodo.'

questions.prompt: Why will the library be closed between the 2nd and the 20th of September?
questions.explanation: The first sentence gives the reason directly: 'encerrada... para obras de modernização' — closed for modernisation works.

questions.prompt: Where can readers return their books during the works?
options: na carrinha ao lado do mercado municipal
na sala de estudo do primeiro andar
no sítio da biblioteca na internet
questions.answer: na carrinha ao lado do mercado municipal
questions.explanation: The mobile library van ('carrinha da biblioteca itinerante') is parked next to the municipal market — the study room and the website are distractors taken from other sentences.

questions.prompt: The text says loans are automatically extended. What does that mean for readers?
options: Não pagam multa se entregarem os livros depois do prazo.
Pagam uma multa pequena a partir de 20 de setembro.
Têm de devolver todos os livros antes do dia 2.
Só podem devolver livros com marcação prévia.
questions.answer: Não pagam multa se entregarem os livros depois do prazo.
questions.explanation: 'Os empréstimos são automaticamente prolongados... não há multas' — no fines. 'Marcação prévia' belongs to the study room, a classic distractor trap.

questions.prompt: Who can use the first-floor study room during the works?
questions.explanation: The study room stays open for students, 'mas apenas com marcação prévia' — only with prior booking through the website.

questions.prompt: Complete the exam instruction (correct): 'Leia o texto com atenção e escolha a opção ___.'
questions.explanation: 'Escolha a opção correta' is the standard instruction on every CAPLE reading task — recognise it instantly on exam day.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'Primeiro leio as perguntas e depois procuro as respostas.'
questions.explanation: The reading strategy in one sentence: questions first ('primeiro'), then ('depois') hunt for the answers in the text.

activities.title: Treino de leitura com a professora
activities.intro: At a café in Coimbra, your teacher checks how your DEPLE reading practice is going. Play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então, como correu o treino de leitura para o DEPLE?
lines.en: So, how did the reading practice for the DEPLE go?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Correu bem! Comecei a ler as perguntas antes do texto e ganho imenso tempo.
lines.en: It went well! I started reading the questions before the text and I save loads of time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa estratégia. E os distratores, já não te enganam?
lines.en: Good strategy. And the distractors, do they still fool you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Às vezes ainda caio: as opções erradas usam as palavras do texto e parecem verdadeiras.
lines.en: Sometimes I still fall for them: the wrong options use words from the text and look true.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Exato, é essa a armadilha. Compara sempre os detalhes: datas, horas e locais.
lines.en: Exactly, that's the trap. Always compare the details: dates, times and places.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vou fazer isso. Amanhã treino com anúncios e cartas — é o que mais sai no exame.
lines.en: I'll do that. Tomorrow I'll practise with ads and letters — that's what comes up most in the exam.

days.title: DEPLE: Produção e Interação Escritas
days.objective: Structure the two DEPLE writing tasks — an informal letter to a friend and a formal message — with the right openings, closings and connectors.
days.paretoFocus: Examiners score structure and register first: a memorised set of openings, closings and four connectors lifts any B1 letter to a passing script, whatever the topic.
drills: Write the skeleton of an informal letter in under five minutes: greeting, three connectors, farewell.
Turn 'Querida Ana' into a formal opener out loud and adjust the closing to match the register.
Link three sentences about your week with 'além disso', 'no entanto' and 'por isso'.

items: Recall one informal and one formal opening formula.
Say the formal closing that ends almost every Portuguese business letter.
Translate: 'In reply to your advertisement, I would like to apply for the job.'

activities.title: Fórmulas de carta e mensagem
activities.intro: The building blocks of the DEPLE writing paper: how to open, connect and close an informal letter and a formal message, in the right register.

items.hr: Querida Ana, espero que esteja tudo bem contigo.
items.en: Dear Ana, I hope everything is well with you.
items.note: informal opening — 'tu' register, for friends and family

items.hr: Exmos. Senhores, venho por este meio pedir informações sobre o curso.
items.en: Dear Sirs, I am writing to request information about the course.
items.note: formal opening + purpose sentence — the pair examiners expect

items.hr: Em resposta ao vosso anúncio, gostaria de me candidatar ao emprego.
items.en: In reply to your advertisement, I would like to apply for the job.
items.note: 'gostaria de' is the written, more formal cousin of 'gostava de'

items.hr: Além disso, o apartamento fica perto do trabalho; por isso, interessa-me muito.
items.en: Moreover, the flat is close to work; therefore, I am very interested.
items.note: 'além disso' adds an argument, 'por isso' draws the conclusion

items.hr: Gosto muito da cidade; no entanto, a renda é demasiado alta.
items.en: I really like the city; however, the rent is far too high.
items.note: 'no entanto' = however — the contrast connector every B1 letter needs

items.hr: Com os melhores cumprimentos, Marta Sousa.
items.en: Best regards, Marta Sousa.
items.note: the standard formal closing in Portugal — never close a formal letter without it

items.hr: Um beijinho e até breve!
items.en: A kiss and see you soon!
items.note: informal closing for friends — never in a formal message

activities.title: Montar a carta perfeita
activities.intro: Choose the right formula for each register and connect the paragraphs — exactly what the DEPLE writing grid rewards.

questions.prompt: You are writing to a company you do not know. Which opening is correct?
questions.explanation: 'Exmos. Senhores' (Excelentíssimos Senhores) is the standard formal opening when you don't know the addressee.

questions.prompt: Which closing matches a formal message?
questions.explanation: Formal letters close with 'Com os melhores cumprimentos'; the other three are informal and would break the register.

questions.prompt: Choose the connector that adds a second argument: 'A casa é barata; ___, fica perto do metro.'
questions.explanation: 'Além disso' = moreover, it stacks a second advantage; 'no entanto' would wrongly signal a contrast.

questions.prompt: Complete with the contrast connector (however): 'O hotel é bonito; ___, o pequeno-almoço é fraco.'
questions.explanation: 'No entanto' introduces the contrast between the nice hotel and the weak breakfast; 'contudo' and 'porém' also work.

questions.prompt: Complete the formal formula: 'Venho por este ___ pedir o reembolso do bilhete.' (means/way)
questions.explanation: 'Venho por este meio + infinitive' — literally 'I come by this means' — is the fixed formal formula for stating your purpose.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'Gostaria de pedir mais informações sobre o curso.'
questions.explanation: 'Gostaria de + infinitive' is the polite written request — the workhorse sentence of any formal DEPLE message.

activities.title: Rever a carta de treino
activities.intro: Your teacher reviews the practice complaint letter you wrote for the DEPLE writing task. Play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Trouxeste a carta de treino? Deixa-me ver a estrutura.
lines.en: Did you bring the practice letter? Let me see the structure.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sim, escrevi uma reclamação formal sobre uma encomenda que nunca chegou.
lines.en: Yes, I wrote a formal complaint about a parcel that never arrived.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Começaste com 'Exmos. Senhores'? E disseste logo o objetivo?
lines.en: Did you start with 'Dear Sirs'? And did you state the purpose straight away?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Comecei, e na primeira frase escrevi: 'venho por este meio apresentar uma reclamação'.
lines.en: I did, and in the first sentence I wrote: 'I hereby wish to make a complaint'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perfeito. E os parágrafos, estão ligados com conetores?
lines.en: Perfect. And the paragraphs, are they linked with connectors?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Estão: usei 'além disso' para o segundo argumento e 'por isso' na conclusão.
lines.en: They are: I used 'moreover' for the second argument and 'therefore' in the conclusion.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Só falta o fecho. Nunca te esqueças: 'Com os melhores cumprimentos' e o teu nome.
lines.en: Only the closing is missing. Never forget: 'Best regards' and your name.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Combinado. Na sexta trago uma carta informal para a Ana, com beijinhos no fim!
lines.en: Deal. On Friday I'll bring an informal letter to Ana, with kisses at the end!

days.title: DEPLE: Compreensão do Oral
days.objective: Face the DEPLE listening paper: predict content from context, and catch the numbers, times and places that decide the answers in announcements and dialogues.
days.paretoFocus: Listening answers hide in tiny details — hours, platforms, prices; training your ear for numbers and 'a partir de' phrases converts exam panic into easy points.
drills: Listen to one minute of any pt-PT audio and note every number, hour and place you hear.
Read today's announcement phrases aloud, then answer from memory: que linha? a que horas?
Predict before listening: imagine an airport announcement and list five words you expect to hear.

items: Recall: which three kinds of details decide most listening answers?
Say the announcement formula for a delayed train, from 'Informamos os passageiros...'
Translate: 'The departure is now scheduled for 10:35 on platform five.'

activities.title: Frases de avisos e mensagens
activities.intro: The stock phrases of Portuguese announcements and recorded messages — the exact language of the DEPLE listening recordings.

items.hr: O comboio Intercidades com destino a Faro vai efetuar a sua partida da linha três.
items.en: The Intercidades train to Faro is about to depart from platform three.
items.note: 'linha' = platform in Portuguese stations — a classic listening detail

items.hr: Última chamada para os passageiros do voo TP 442 com destino a Paris.
items.en: Last call for passengers on flight TP 442 to Paris.
items.note: airport announcements: flight number + destination + gate

items.hr: Devido a uma avaria, o elevador do metro está fora de serviço.
items.en: Due to a breakdown, the metro lift is out of service.
items.note: 'devido a' introduces the reason — questions love asking porquê

items.hr: A consulta do senhor Silva foi transferida para quinta-feira, às 15h30.
items.en: Mr Silva's appointment has been moved to Thursday at 3:30 pm.
items.note: phone messages: catch the new day AND the new time

items.hr: Está a ligar para a Clínica Boa Saúde. Para marcações, prima um.
items.en: You have reached the Boa Saúde clinic. For appointments, press one.
items.note: recorded menus — 'prima' is the formal imperative of premir (to press)

items.hr: Antes de ouvir, leia as perguntas e tente prever as palavras que vão aparecer.
items.en: Before listening, read the questions and try to predict the words that will come up.
items.note: prediction strategy — your brain hears what it expects

items.hr: Os números dizem-se depressa: setenta e sete não é sessenta e sete.
items.en: Numbers go by fast: seventy-seven is not sixty-seven.
items.note: similar-sounding numbers are the exam's favourite trap

activities.title: Compreensão do oral tipo DEPLE
activities.intro: Read this station announcement as if you had just heard it once, then answer from the details. TRANSCRIPT: 'Bom dia. Informamos os passageiros de que o comboio Alfa Pendular das 10h15 com destino ao Porto — Campanhã se encontra atrasado cerca de vinte minutos. A saída está agora prevista para as 10h35, na linha cinco. Os passageiros com bilhete para este comboio podem, se preferirem, viajar no Intercidades das 10h50, sem custos adicionais, mediante apresentação do bilhete. Lembramos ainda que amanhã, sábado, os comboios urbanos de Sintra funcionam com horário reduzido devido a trabalhos na via. Para mais informações, dirija-se ao balcão de apoio ao cliente.'

questions.prompt: How late is the 10:15 Alfa Pendular to Porto?
questions.explanation: 'Atrasado cerca de vinte minutos' — about twenty minutes late; the exam expects you to catch the exact number.

questions.prompt: From which platform will the train now leave, and at what time?
questions.explanation: New departure: 10h35 on platform five. 10h15 is the old time and 10h50 is the OTHER train — both classic number traps.

questions.prompt: What can passengers with a ticket for the delayed train do?
options: viajar no Intercidades das 10h50 sem pagar mais
pedir o reembolso total na linha cinco
apanhar um autocarro gratuito para o Porto
questions.answer: viajar no Intercidades das 10h50 sem pagar mais
questions.explanation: 'Podem viajar no Intercidades das 10h50, sem custos adicionais' — they may take the 10:50 train at no extra cost, just by showing their ticket.

questions.prompt: What happens tomorrow on the Sintra line?
options: os comboios funcionam com horário reduzido
há comboios extra ao fim de semana
as viagens passam a ser gratuitas
questions.answer: os comboios funcionam com horário reduzido
questions.explanation: 'Os comboios urbanos de Sintra funcionam com horário reduzido devido a trabalhos na via' — a reduced timetable because of track works.

questions.prompt: Complete the announcement formula (departure): 'O comboio vai efetuar a sua ___ da linha três.'
questions.explanation: 'Efetuar a sua partida' is the fixed station formula for departing — learn it as one chunk so it never surprises your ear.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'Para mais informações, ligue para o apoio ao cliente.'
questions.explanation: The standard closing line of Portuguese announcements — recognising it tells you the recording is about to end.

activities.title: No balcão de apoio ao cliente
activities.intro: You heard the station announcement but missed some details. Play 'Me' and confirm them at the customer support desk.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bom dia, apoio ao cliente. Faça favor.
lines.en: Good morning, customer support. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bom dia. Ouvi o aviso, mas não percebi bem: o Alfa das 10h15 parte de que linha?
lines.en: Good morning. I heard the announcement but didn't quite catch it: which platform does the 10:15 Alfa leave from?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Da linha cinco, às 10h35. Está atrasado cerca de vinte minutos.
lines.en: From platform five, at 10:35. It's about twenty minutes late.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E posso usar o meu bilhete no Intercidades das 10h50?
lines.en: And can I use my ticket on the 10:50 Intercidades?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pode, sem custos adicionais. Basta mostrar o bilhete ao revisor.
lines.en: You can, at no extra cost. Just show your ticket to the inspector.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ótimo. Amanhã também viajo para Sintra — há algum problema na linha?
lines.en: Great. Tomorrow I'm also travelling to Sintra — is there any problem on the line?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Há trabalhos na via, por isso os urbanos funcionam com horário reduzido.
lines.en: There are track works, so the suburban trains run on a reduced timetable.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Muito obrigado pela ajuda. Vou já para a linha cinco!
lines.en: Thank you very much for your help. I'm heading straight to platform five!

days.title: DEPLE: Produção e Interação Orais
days.objective: Handle the DEPLE oral: deliver a structured self-presentation, describe past experiences, and manage the interaction — asking for repetition and giving opinions politely.
days.paretoFocus: Examiners reward candidates who keep talking: a rehearsed presentation skeleton plus three repair phrases ('pode repetir?', 'se bem percebi...') keep the conversation alive under pressure.
drills: Time yourself: a two-minute 'apresentação pessoal' — name, origin, work, hobbies, why Portuguese.
Describe your last trip in the past tense, using 'primeiro', 'depois' and 'no fim'.
Repeat three repair phrases aloud until they are automatic: 'Desculpe, pode repetir, por favor?'

items: Recall the skeleton of the apresentação pessoal: who, where from, what you do, why Portuguese.
Say two polite ways to give an opinion in Portuguese.
Translate: 'Sorry, could you repeat the question, please?'

activities.title: Frases para a prova oral
activities.intro: The oral exam has two engines: a structured monologue (presentation, experiences) and interaction phrases that keep the dialogue going when you hesitate.

items.hr: Chamo-me Marta, sou francesa e vivo em Lisboa há três anos.
items.en: My name is Marta, I'm French and I've been living in Lisbon for three years.
items.note: opening line: name + origin + time in Portugal — note the ênclise 'chamo-me'

items.hr: Trabalho numa empresa de turismo e nos tempos livres faço caminhadas.
items.en: I work at a tourism company and in my free time I go hiking.
items.note: work + hobbies — the second block of the presentation

items.hr: Uma experiência que me marcou foi a minha primeira viagem ao Alentejo.
items.en: An experience that marked me was my first trip to the Alentejo.
items.note: launchpad sentence for describing past experiences

items.hr: Desculpe, pode repetir a pergunta, por favor? Não percebi bem.
items.en: Sorry, could you repeat the question, please? I didn't quite understand.
items.note: asking for repetition costs nothing — silence costs points

items.hr: Se bem percebi, quer que eu fale sobre os meus planos, certo?
items.en: If I understood correctly, you want me to talk about my plans, right?
items.note: confirming the task buys precious thinking time

items.hr: Na minha opinião, viver numa cidade pequena tem mais vantagens.
items.en: In my opinion, living in a small town has more advantages.
items.note: 'na minha opinião' — the safest opinion opener

items.hr: Concordo em parte, mas acho que depende da situação.
items.en: I partly agree, but I think it depends on the situation.
items.note: polite partial disagreement — B1 gold in the interaction task

activities.title: Sobreviver à prova oral
activities.intro: Choose or build the phrase that keeps the oral exam moving — presentation lines, repair phrases and polite opinions.

questions.prompt: The examiner speaks fast and you miss the question. What do you say?
options: Desculpe, pode repetir a pergunta, por favor?
Nada — é melhor ficar em silêncio.
questions.answer: Desculpe, pode repetir a pergunta, por favor?
questions.explanation: Politely asking for repetition is expected and costs nothing; silence or rudeness loses interaction marks.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct European Portuguese opener for the personal presentation:
options: Chamo-me Marta e vivo em Lisboa há três anos.
Me chamo Marta e vivo em Lisboa há três anos.
Eu ser a Marta de Lisboa.
questions.answer: Chamo-me Marta e vivo em Lisboa há três anos.
questions.explanation: European Portuguese uses ênclise in affirmative main clauses: 'chamo-me', never sentence-initial 'me chamo' (that próclise is Brazilian).

questions.prompt: How do you give an opinion politely in the interaction task?
options: Na minha opinião, o campo é mais sossegado.
questions.answer: Na minha opinião, o campo é mais sossegado.
questions.explanation: 'Na minha opinião' frames a personal view politely; the other options are abrupt or rude and would hurt the interaction score.

questions.prompt: Complete the confirmation phrase (understood): 'Se bem ___, quer que eu descreva a minha cidade.'
questions.explanation: 'Se bem percebi' = if I understood correctly — it confirms the task and buys you time to think.

questions.prompt: Complete (marked me): 'Uma experiência que me ___ foi o primeiro dia de trabalho no Porto.'
questions.explanation: 'Uma experiência que me marcou' — an experience that marked me — is the classic launchpad for the past-experience monologue.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'Concordo em parte, mas depende da situação.'
questions.explanation: Polite partial agreement: 'concordo em parte' + 'mas' + your nuance — a high-scoring interaction move.

activities.title: Ensaio da prova oral
activities.intro: A realistic DEPLE oral rehearsal: the examiner (Partner) leads, you present yourself, describe an experience and give an opinion. Play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bom dia. Sente-se, por favor. Como se chama e de onde é?
lines.en: Good morning. Please sit down. What is your name and where are you from?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bom dia. Chamo-me Marta, sou francesa e vivo em Lisboa há três anos.
lines.en: Good morning. My name is Marta, I'm French and I've been living in Lisbon for three years.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Muito bem. Fale-me um pouco do seu dia a dia.
lines.en: Very good. Tell me a little about your daily life.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Trabalho numa empresa de turismo; ao fim do dia vou ao ginásio e, ao fim de semana, gosto de ir à praia.
lines.en: I work at a tourism company; at the end of the day I go to the gym and, at weekends, I like going to the beach.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Conte-me uma experiência que a tenha marcado em Portugal.
lines.en: Tell me about an experience that has marked you in Portugal.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Desculpe, pode repetir a pergunta, por favor?
lines.en: Sorry, could you repeat the question, please?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Com certeza: uma experiência importante que viveu cá em Portugal.
lines.en: Of course: an important experience you have had here in Portugal.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ah, sim! A minha primeira viagem ao Alentejo: primeiro visitei Évora, depois provei a gastronomia e, no fim, não queria voltar.
lines.en: Ah, yes! My first trip to the Alentejo: first I visited Évora, then I tried the food and, in the end, I didn't want to come back.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E na sua opinião, é melhor viver em Lisboa ou numa cidade pequena?
lines.en: And in your opinion, is it better to live in Lisbon or in a small town?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Na minha opinião, depende: Lisboa tem mais oportunidades; no entanto, uma cidade pequena é mais sossegada.
lines.en: In my opinion, it depends: Lisbon has more opportunities; however, a small town is quieter.

days.title: Simulação DEPLE — Ponto de controlo B1
days.objective: Run a mini DEPLE mock: know the exam-day format and pass rule cold, answer mixed exam-style questions, and get through a full oral simulation.
days.paretoFocus: Knowing the rules is free marks in disguise: four equal components and a 55% global average mean a strong reading day can carry a weaker listening day — plan your effort accordingly.
drills: Recite the four DEPLE components from memory, in Portuguese, with their equal weight.
Do today's exercise against the clock: ninety seconds per question, no dictionary.
Record your two-minute apresentação pessoal and listen back for missing connectors.

items: Recall the pass rule: what global average do you need, and is there a per-section minimum?
Say the four classification bands, from Muito Bom down to Insuficiente.
Translate: 'Each component is worth 25% of the final mark.'

activities.title: O dia do exame DEPLE
activities.intro: The exam-day rundown: what the DEPLE consists of, how it is weighted, and exactly what you need to pass — verified against the CAPLE rules.

items.hr: O DEPLE tem quatro componentes: Compreensão da Leitura, Produção e Interação Escritas, Compreensão do Oral e Produção e Interação Orais.
items.en: The DEPLE has four components: Reading, Writing, Listening and Speaking.
items.note: the B1 certificate of CAPLE, Universidade de Lisboa

items.hr: As quatro componentes têm peso igual: cada uma vale 25% da nota final.
items.en: The four components carry equal weight: each one is worth 25% of the final mark.
items.note: no component matters more than another — train them all

items.hr: A classificação final é uma média global — não há nota mínima por componente.
items.en: The final classification is a global average — there is no minimum mark per component.
items.note: a strong paper can compensate a weaker one

items.hr: Para passar, precisa de Suficiente: uma média global de, pelo menos, 55%.
items.en: To pass you need 'Suficiente': a global average of at least 55%.
items.note: Suficiente covers 55%–69% — the pass threshold

items.hr: Bom corresponde a 70%–84% e Muito Bom a 85%–100%.
items.en: 'Bom' corresponds to 70–84% and 'Muito Bom' to 85–100%.
items.note: the two bands above the pass mark

items.hr: Abaixo de 55%, a classificação é Insuficiente e o exame não é superado.
items.en: Below 55%, the classification is 'Insuficiente' and the exam is not passed.
items.note: the fail band — everything under the 55% average

items.hr: No dia do exame, leve um documento de identificação e chegue com antecedência.
items.en: On exam day, bring an identification document and arrive early.
items.note: practical exam-day logistics — no document, no exam

activities.title: Mini-simulação DEPLE
activities.intro: Mixed exam-style questions: reading on a short notice plus usage. TEXT: 'AVISO — Junta de Freguesia de Santa Clara: no próximo sábado, dia 12, realiza-se a feira de produtos regionais no largo da igreja, das 9h às 18h. Devido à feira, o estacionamento no largo estará proibido desde sexta-feira à noite. Os moradores podem estacionar gratuitamente no parque da escola. Em caso de chuva, a feira passa para o pavilhão municipal.'

questions.prompt: According to the notice, where can residents park during the fair?
options: em frente à junta de freguesia
questions.explanation: 'Os moradores podem estacionar gratuitamente no parque da escola' — the square is banned and the pavilion is only the rain plan.

questions.prompt: From when is parking banned on the square?
questions.explanation: Distractor trap: the fair is on Saturday, but the ban starts 'desde sexta-feira à noite' — Friday night, one day earlier.

questions.prompt: What happens if it rains on Saturday?
options: a feira muda para o pavilhão municipal
questions.answer: a feira muda para o pavilhão municipal
questions.explanation: 'Em caso de chuva, a feira passa para o pavilhão municipal' — it moves indoors, it is not cancelled or postponed.

questions.prompt: Usage check — choose the correct European Portuguese form: 'Neste momento, estou ___ estudar para o DEPLE.'
questions.explanation: European Portuguese uses 'estar a + infinitivo' for actions in progress — 'estou a estudar', never the gerúndio progressive.

questions.prompt: Complete with the connector meaning 'therefore': 'O estacionamento está proibido no largo; ___, os moradores usam o parque da escola.'
questions.explanation: 'Por isso' draws the logical conclusion — the same connector that structures your DEPLE writing answers.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'A nota final é a média das quatro componentes.'
questions.explanation: The DEPLE pass rule in one sentence: the final mark is the average of the four equally weighted components.

activities.title: Simulação da prova oral
activities.intro: The full oral-exam simulation: the examiner (Partner) runs the real sequence — presentation, description, plans and opinion. Play 'Me' as on exam day.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa tarde. Esta é a prova de Produção e Interação Orais. Apresente-se, por favor.
lines.en: Good afternoon. This is the Speaking test. Please introduce yourself.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Boa tarde. Chamo-me Marta, sou francesa, vivo em Lisboa há três anos e trabalho em turismo.
lines.en: Good afternoon. My name is Marta, I'm French, I've been living in Lisbon for three years and I work in tourism.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Obrigado. Descreva-me um fim de semana típico seu.
lines.en: Thank you. Describe a typical weekend of yours.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ao sábado vou ao mercado e almoço com amigos; ao domingo, se está bom tempo, apanhamos o comboio para Cascais.
lines.en: On Saturdays I go to the market and have lunch with friends; on Sundays, if the weather is good, we take the train to Cascais.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Interessante. E que planos tem para o futuro?
lines.en: Interesting. And what plans do you have for the future?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se bem percebi, quer saber os meus planos profissionais? Gostava de abrir uma pequena agência de turismo no Porto.
lines.en: If I understood correctly, you want to know my professional plans? I'd like to open a small tourism agency in Porto.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Exatamente. Última questão: na sua opinião, o turismo faz bem às cidades portuguesas?
lines.en: Exactly. Last question: in your opinion, is tourism good for Portuguese cities?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Na minha opinião, traz emprego e vida aos bairros; no entanto, também sobe as rendas — por isso é preciso equilíbrio.
lines.en: In my opinion, it brings jobs and life to the neighbourhoods; however, it also pushes rents up — so balance is needed.
